Peak shifts reveal systematic unit cell expansion or contraction, often due to dopant substitution or lattice strains.
Suppressed or missing peaks denote low crystallite size/crystallinity, or highly oriented sample alignment.
Atypical peaks point to unreacted precursors, secondary reaction pathways or organic mineral contaminants.
